title: silene ruscifolia
intro: Silene ruscifolia, also known as corn chamomile or rough-seed catchfly, is a flowering plant in the caryophyllaceae family. The plant is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce pain and swelling, and promote wound healing. Silene ruscifolia is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and supporting respiratory health. Herbal preparations of Silene ruscifolia can be made by infusing the plant's leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's roots to make a tincture or extract.
